display.purpose: Page-level titles and large KPI labels. One per screen.

headline.purpose: Section headers, dialog titles, primary panel headings.

title-sm.purpose: Table column headers, form section labels.

body.purpose: All prose, descriptions, table cell data. The default.

label.purpose: Helper text, input labels, supplementary metadata.

caption.purpose: Status badge text, system labels, tag overlines (uppercase only).

kpi.purpose: Dashboard metric numeric values exclusively. Never repurposed.

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: Default card and panel lift. Prevents edge loss on bright displays.

shadows.name: modal
shadows.purpose: Full modal dialogs only. Maximum depth in the system.

motion.name: state-change
motion.purpose: Default duration for all state transitions (color, border, bg).

breakpoints.name: sidebar-collapse
breakpoints.purpose: Sidebar switches from 240px to 64px icon-only below this.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Single-column stack. Sidebar becomes a bottom sheet or drawer.

components.name: Button Primary
components.description: Primary CTA. Used for the single most important action on a screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #2563EB; color: #fff; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.6; border: none; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1D4ED8; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #DBEAFE; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #1E40AF; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: #CBD5E1; color: #94A3B8; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Button Secondary
components.description: Secondary action. Paired with a primary button or used for lower-priority actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #2563EB; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.6; border: 1px solid #2563EB; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #EFF6FF; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #DBEAFE; } .ds-btn-secondary:disabled { color: #94A3B8; border-color: #CBD5E1; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Button Danger
components.description: Destructive action only. Delete appointment, cancel comanda. Never for warnings.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { background: #EF4444; color: #fff; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.6; border: none; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { background: #DC2626; } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #FEE2E2; } .ds-btn-danger:active { background: #B91C1C; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Standard text input with label. Used across all forms.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-label { font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.5; color: #64748B; } .ds-input { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E2E8F0; border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.6; color: #0F172A; transition: border-color 150ms ease-out, box-shadow 150ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #94A3B8; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #2563EB; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px #DBEAFE; } .ds-input:disabled { background: #F1F5F9; color: #CBD5E1; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Appointment state indicator. All 8 lifecycle states. Always color + icon + label.
components.html: CONFIRMADO
components.css: .ds-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; padding: 4px 8px; border-radius: 4px; border: 1px solid; font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-status--scheduled { background: #EFF6FF; color: #1D4ED8; border-color: #2563EB; } .ds-status--confirmed { background: #D1FAE5; color: #065F46; border-color: #10B981; } .ds-status--checkin { background: #FEF3C7; color: #92400E; border-color: #F59E0B; } .ds-status--inservice { background: #F3E8FF; color: #6B21A8; border-color: #8B5CF6; } .ds-status--awaiting { background: #FEE2E2; color: #991B1B; border-color: #EF4444; } .ds-status--completed { background: #D1FAE5; color: #065F46; border-color: #10B981; } .ds-status--noshow { background: #F1F5F9; color: #475569; border-color: #94A3B8; } .ds-status--cancelled { background: #FEE2E2; color: #991B1B; border-color: #EF4444; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Standard container for grouped content. Cards are never nested.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E2E8F0; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.04); padding: 16px; } .ds-card__title { font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; color: #0F172A; margin: 0 0 4px; } .ds-card__body { font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.6; color: #64748B; margin: 0; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Navigation item inside the Command Dark sidebar. Shows idle, hover, and active states.
components.html: AgendaClientes
components.css: .ds-sidebar { background: #0F172A; padding: 8px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 6px; font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 400; color: #94A3B8; text-decoration: none; transition: background 150ms ease-out, color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #1E293B; color: #CBD5E1; } .ds-nav-item--active { background: #2563EB; color: #FFFFFF; font-weight: 500; }

components.name: KPI Tile
components.description: Dashboard metric display. Numeric value + label only. Never repurposed for marketing.
components.css: .ds-kpi-card { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E2E8F0; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.04); padding: 24px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-kpi__value { font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 28px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.1; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: #0F172A; } .ds-kpi__label { font-family: 'SF Pro Text', Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #64748B; }

narrative.overview:
The Milli Agenda design system exists to make a salon's operational tempo visible and actionable — at a glance, under pressure, with one hand free. Like the clock on the salon wall, every element must be instantly readable: what state, whose chair, what needs doing next. The aesthetic borrows directly from Stripe's operational density — high information, zero visual noise — and applies it to the rhythm of a Brazilian beauty business where a missed appointment or an unclear status costs real money.

This system explicitly rejects the glossy marketplace UI of Fresha and Booksy, where saturated conversion CTAs displace professional workflow. It rejects the gradient-and-glassmorphism dashboard aesthetic that reads as a Dribbble demo on first load and breaks under daily use. It equally rejects the generic teal-and-white SaaS neutral that achieves clarity by erasing context.

Design serves the shift, not the brand. The interface disappears while the work happens.
keyCharacteristics: Operational density: Stripe-level information per screen-centimeter
Signal Blue used only for decision points — primary actions, active selections, focused fields
Eight appointment states, each uniquely identifiable by color + icon + label (never color alone)
Sidebar as command layer: dark, authoritative, always-collapse-aware
Flat surfaces by default; shadows mark layer relationships, not aesthetics

rules.name: The One-Accent Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue (#2563EB) is the only saturated accent in the neutral field. Status colors encode lifecycle state, not personality. The moment Signal Blue appears as decoration, the system loses its primary decision signal.

rules.name: The No-Decoration Rule
rules.body: Danger red, success green, warning amber, and purple each map to specific appointment lifecycle states. Using them for badges, promotions, or visual accent corrupts the operational vocabulary.

rules.name: The No-Fluid Rule
rules.body: All font sizes are fixed in px. Product UIs at consistent DPI gain nothing from clamp() type and lose layout predictability.

rules.name: The KPI-Only Rule
rules.body: The 28px/700 role is exclusive to dashboard metric values. A large bold number in any other context is the hero-metric SaaS cliché: prohibited.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces rest flat. Shadows appear only to mark functional layer relationships. No ambient glows, no blur, no glassmorphism under any circumstance.

dos: Do use Signal Blue (#2563EB) exclusively for primary CTA buttons, active sidebar nav, focused input borders, and selection indicators.
Do always render appointment status with color + icon + text label together. WCAG AA forbids color as the sole state indicator.
Do use fixed px sizes for all typography. No clamp() in product UI.
Do use only the three-shadow vocabulary (card / dropdown / modal).
Do target ≤ 3 interactions for any critical operation: schedule, check-in, charge.
Do prefer density over whitespace when density reduces navigation.
Do keep all motion to 150ms. Transitions convey state; anything longer performs for the user.
donts: Don't reproduce Fresha or Booksy's marketplace UI patterns — saturated Book Now CTAs, consumer star ratings, promotional banners.
Don't use glassmorphism. Background blurs, frosted panels, and translucent cards are explicitly banned.
Don't use the startup gradient-dashboard aesthetic: glowing metric cards, gradient overlays, neon accent pulses.
Don't produce generic SaaS neutral. Milli Agenda is a Brazilian salon operations tool with a specific vocabulary.
Don't use display fonts in UI labels, data cells, buttons, or navigation.
Don't let semantic colors appear as decoration. Each maps to a specific appointment lifecycle state.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text + gradient). Never intentional.
Don't add page-load choreography. Motion conveys state change only.
Don't use border-left/border-right > 1px as a colored decorative stripe. Use full-width background blocks instead.
